item.title: Access Google sheets in Javascript
item.description: This blog talks about setting up a google client project and access google sheets in vanilla javascript.

item.title: Install spack
item.description: git clone --depth=2 --branch=releases/v1.1 github.com/spack/spack.git ~/spack source ~/spack/share/spack/setup-env.sh # Ensure that this fine gcc@11.4 as compiler spack compiler find # You can remove any extra compilers found by above command # by running: # spack compiler rm <compiler spec> s...

item.title: Slurm srun command
item.description: srun command is used to run task on node. It provides options to provide partition, nodes and other resources.

item.title: one-definition Rule (ODR)
item.description: The one-definition rule (ODR) is a rule in C++ which contains three parts: In a file, each function, variable, type or template in a given scope should only have one definition.

item.title: Header files in C++
item.description: Header files contain a bunch of forward declarations that can be used in multiple c++ source code.

item.title: Conditional Compilation in C++
item.description: Conditional compilation allows to conditional define what instructions can be compiled and what to skip.

item.title: define directive in C++
item.description: Macros are used to define identifiers which can then be replaced the text they represent.

item.title: #include directive
item.description: When processor encounters #include directive, it replaces directive with the content of the included file.

item.title: Preprocessors in C++
item.description: Preprocessor are one step before the actual compilation. It does works like removing the comments ensuring there are new lines at the file end.

item.title: Namespaces in C++
item.description: A namespace is a scope region which contains names. A namespace identifies where the particular name (a function, types etc).
